title: Align onboarding known-issues matching with canonical stack names
description: Tighten `src/agent_takt/onboarding/scaffold.py` so `_language_specific_known_issues()` matches the canonical stack names produced by the new prompt flow and covers the Go case expected by the spec.
acceptance_criteria: Known-issues logic uses exact canonical names rather than fuzzy substring matching for Node.js and TypeScript.
Canonical `Node.js` and `TypeScript` return the expected language-specific guidance block.
Go-specific guidance is present if required by existing behavior gaps described in the spec.

handoff_summary.completed: Tightened `_language_specific_known_issues()` in `src/agent_takt/onboarding/scaffold.py` to use exact canonical name matching instead of fuzzy `in lang_lower` substring checks. The function now matches `"Node.js"` and `"TypeScript"` for the TypeScript/Node.js guidance block, `"Go"` for the Go block, and returns `""` for all other values including free-text "Other" fallbacks.
handoff_summary.remaining: N/A — the bead scope is fully addressed.
handoff_summary.risks: None. The change is strictly narrowing: exact matching replaces fuzzy matching. Callers passing canonical STACKS display names (the only path after B-7e28a7b0's prompt refactor) behave identically. Free-text values from the "Other" fallback path now correctly return no guidance instead of accidentally matching (e.g. "My Node.js App" used to match; now it does not).
handoff_summary.next_action: Tester agent should verify: (1) `_language_specific_known_issues("Node.js")` returns the TypeScript/Node.js block; (2) `_language_specific_known_issues("TypeScript")` returns the same block; (3) `_language_specific_known_issues("Other")` returns `""`; (4) `_language_specific_known_issues("Go")` returns the Go block; (5) free-text values like `"node"`, `"nodejs"`, `"typescript stuff"` no longer match.
handoff_summary.conflict_risks: None — only `scaffold.py` was modified; no overlap with other in-progress beads.
handoff_summary.design_decisions: Chose exact string equality (`language in ("Node.js", "TypeScript")`, `language == "Go"`) over a set lookup or imported STACKS reference. This keeps the function self-contained, avoids a circular import risk, and makes the accepted values explicit in the code. An alternative of importing STACKS and deriving matches dynamically was rejected as over-engineering for a 3-branch lookup. The docstring now explicitly documents the contract so future maintainers understand why there is no `.lower()` normalization.
handoff_summary.test_coverage_notes: Test cases needed: `_language_specific_known_issues` with each canonical STACKS display name that should match ("Node.js", "TypeScript", "Go"), with "Other" (must return ""), with Python/Rust/Java(Maven) (must return ""), and with plausible free-text values that the old fuzzy logic would have incorrectly matched ("node", "nodejs", "typescript/react", "golang"). Also confirm `seed_memory_files` produces a known-issues file that includes the Go block when `answers.language == "Go"`.
handoff_summary.known_limitations: N/A — implementation is complete as specified. Languages added to STACKS in the future (e.g. Rust, Java) will silently return no language-specific guidance until someone adds a branch here; this is acceptable per spec scope.

conflict_risks: None — only `scaffold.py` was modified; no overlap with other in-progress beads.

execution_history.summary: Replaced fuzzy substring matching in `_language_specific_known_issues()` with exact canonical name matching against the STACKS catalog display names.
